   165→# =============================================================================
   166→# Tokenizer
   167→# =============================================================================
   168→
   169→class TokenType(Enum):
   170→    TASK = "TASK"
   171→    IDENTIFIER = "IDENTIFIER"
   172→    LBRACE = "LBRACE"
   173→    RBRACE = "RBRACE"
   174→    LBRACKET = "LBRACKET"
   175→    RBRACKET = "RBRACKET"
   176→    LPAREN = "LPAREN"
   177→    RPAREN = "RPAREN"
   178→    COLON = "COLON"
   179→    COMMA = "COMMA"
   180→    SEMICOLON = "SEMICOLON"
   181→    ASSIGN = "ASSIGN"
   182→    STRING = "STRING"
   183→    NUMBER = "NUMBER"
   184→    BOOL = "BOOL"
   185→    PARAM_TYPE = "PARAM_TYPE"
   186→    EQUALS = "EQUALS"
   187→    PERCENT = "PERCENT"
   188→    FOR = "FOR"
   189→    WHILE = "WHILE"
   190→    IF = "IF"
   191→    ELIF = "ELIF"
   192→    ELSE = "ELSE"
   193→    RETURN = "RETURN"
   194→    BREAK = "BREAK"
   195→    CONTINUE = "CONTINUE"
   196→    IN = "IN"
   197→    DOT = "DOT"
   198→    NOT = "NOT"
   199→    AND = "AND"
   200→    OR = "OR"
   201→    LT = "LT"
   202→    GT = "GT"
   203→    LTE = "LTE"
   204→    GTE = "GTE"
   205→    EQ = "EQ"
   206→    NEQ = "NEQ"
   207→    PLUS = "PLUS"
   208→    MINUS = "MINUS"
   209→    MULT = "MULT"
   210→    DIV = "DIV"
   211→    MOD = "MOD"
   212→    EOF = "EOF"
   213→
   214→
   215→@dataclass
   216→class Token:
   217→    type: TokenType
   218→    value: str
   219→    line: int
   220→    column: int
   221→
   222→
   223→class Tokenizer:
   224→    KEYWORDS = {
   225→        "task": TokenType.TASK,
   226→        "run": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,  # treated as identifier
   227→        "success": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   228→        "requires": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   229→        "params": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   230→        "output": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   231→        "timeout": TokenType.IDENTIFIER,
   232→        "for": TokenType.FOR,
   233→        "while": TokenType.WHILE,
   234→        "if": TokenType.IF,
   235→        "elif": TokenType.ELIF,
   236→        "else": TokenType.ELSE,
   237→        "return": TokenType.RETURN,
   238→        "break": TokenType.BREAK,
   239→        "continue": TokenType.CONTINUE,
   240→        "TRUE": TokenType.BOOL,
   241→        "FALSE": TokenType.BOOL,
   242→        "string": TokenType.PARAM_TYPE,
   243→        "int": TokenType.PARAM_TYPE,
   244→        "float": TokenType.PARAM_TYPE,
   245→        "bool": TokenType.PARAM_TYPE,
   246→        "list": TokenType.PARAM_TYPE,
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    def __init__(self, source: str):
   250→        self.source = source
   251→        self.pos = 0
   252→        self.line = 1
   253→        self.column = 1
   254→        self.tokens: List[Token] = []
   255→
   256→    def tokenize(self) -> List[Token]:
   257→        while self.pos < len(self.source):
   258→            char = self.source[self.pos]
   259→
   260→            if char.isspace():
   261→                self._handle_whitespace(char)
   262→            elif char == '{':
   263→                self._add_token(TokenType.LBRACE, '{')
   264→            elif char == '}':
   265→                self._add_token(TokenType.RBRACE, '}')
   266→            elif char == '[':
   267→                self._add_token(TokenType.LBRACKET, '[')
   268→            elif char == ']':
   269→                self._add_token(TokenType.RBRACKET, ']')
   270→            elif char == '(':
   271→                self._add_token(TokenType.LPAREN, '(')
   272→            elif char == ')':
   273→                self._add_token(TokenType.RPAREN, ')')
   274→            elif char == ':':
   275→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '=':
   276→                    self._add_token(TokenType.EQUALS, ':=')
   277→                    self.pos += 1
   278→                    self.column += 1
   279→                else:
   280→                    self._add_token(TokenType.COLON, ':')
   281→            elif char == ',':
   282→                self._add_token(TokenType.COMMA, ',')
   283→            elif char == ';':
   284→                self._add_token(TokenType.SEMICOLON, ';')
   285→            elif char == '%':
   286→                self._add_token(TokenType.PERCENT, '%')
   287→            elif char == '!':
   288→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '=':
   289→                    self._add_token(TokenType.NEQ, '!=')
   290→                    self.pos += 1
   291→                    self.column += 1
   292→                else:
   293→                    self._add_token(TokenType.NOT, '!')
   294→            elif char == '<':
   295→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '=':
   296→                    self._add_token(TokenType.LTE, '<=')
   297→                    self.pos += 1
   298→                    self.column += 1
   299→                else:
   300→                    self._add_token(TokenType.LT, '<')
   301→            elif char == '>':
   302→                # Check for >> (double greater than) before >=
   303→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '>':
   304→                    self._add_token(TokenType.STRING, '>>')
   305→                    self.pos += 1
   306→                    self.column += 1
   307→                elif self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '=':
   308→                    self._add_token(TokenType.GTE, '>=')
   309→                    self.pos += 1
   310→                    self.column += 1
   311→                else:
   312→                    self._add_token(TokenType.GT, '>')
   313→            elif char == '=':
   314→                if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '=':
   315→                    self._add_token(TokenType.EQ, '==')
   316→                    self.pos += 1
   317→                    self.column += 1
   318→                else:
   319→                    self._add_token(TokenType.ASSIGN, '=')
   320→            elif char == '+':
   321→                self._add_token(TokenType.PLUS, '+')
   322→            elif char == '-':
   323→                self._add_token(TokenType.MINUS, '-')
   324→            elif char == '*':
   325→                self._add_token(TokenType.MULT, '*')
   326→            elif char == '/':
   327→                self._add_token(TokenType.DIV, '/')
   328→            elif char == '"' or char == "'":
   329→                self._handle_string(char)
   330→            elif char == '$':
   331→                self._handle_dollar()
   332→            elif char.isalpha() or char == '_':
   333→                self._handle_identifier()
   334→            elif char.isdigit() or char == '.':
   335→                self._handle_number()
   336→            else:
   337→                raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected character '{char}' at line {self.line}")
   338→
   339→        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.EOF, '', self.line, self.column))
   340→        return self.tokens
   341→
   342→    def _handle_whitespace(self, char: str):
   343→        if char == '\n':
   344→            self.line += 1
   345→            self.column = 1
   346→        else:
   347→            self.column += 1
   348→        self.pos += 1
   349→
   350→    def _handle_string(self, quote: str):
   351→        start = self.pos
   352→        self.pos += 1
   353→        self.column += 1
   354→        result = []
   355→
   356→        while self.pos < len(self.source):
   357→            char = self.source[self.pos]
   358→            if char == '\\':
   359→                # Handle escape sequences
   360→                self.pos += 1
   361→                self.column += 1
   362→                if self.pos >= len(self.source):
   363→                    break
   364→                escape_char = self.source[self.pos]
   365→                escape_map = {
   366→                    'n': '\n',
   367→                    't': '\t',
   368→                    'r': '\r',
   369→                    '"': '"',
   370→                    "'": "'",
   371→                    '\\': '\\',
   372→                }
   373→                result.append(escape_map.get(escape_char, escape_char))
   374→            elif char == quote:
   375→                break
   376→            else:
   377→                result.append(char)
   378→            self.pos += 1
   379→            self.column += 1
   380→
   381→        if self.pos >= len(self.source) or self.source[self.pos] != quote:
   382→            raise SyntaxError(f"Unterminated string at line {self.line}")
   383→
   384→        self.pos += 1
   385→        self.column += 1
   386→        value = ''.join(result)
   387→        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STRING, value, self.line, start))
   388→
   389→    def _handle_identifier(self):
   390→        start = self.pos
   391→        while self.pos < len(self.source) and (self.source[self.pos].isalnum() or self.source[self.pos] == '_'):
   392→            self.pos += 1
   393→            self.column += 1
   394→
   395→        value = self.source[start:self.pos]
   396→        token_type = self.KEYWORDS.get(value.lower(), TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
   397→        self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, self.line, start))
   398→
   399→    def _handle_dollar(self):
   400→        """Handle ${params.x} and ${workspace} patterns"""
   401→        if self.pos + 1 < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos + 1] == '{':
   402→            # Found ${...}
   403→            start = self.pos
   404→            self.pos += 2  # Skip ${"
   405→            self.column += 2
   406→            var_name = []
   407→            while self.pos < len(self.source) and self.source[self.pos] != '}':
   408→                var_name.append(self.source[self.pos])
   409→                self.pos += 1
   410→                self.column += 1
   411→
   412→            if self.pos >= len(self.source) or self.source[self.pos] != '}':
   413→                raise SyntaxError("Unterminated ${ at line " + str(self.line))
   414→
   415→            self.pos += 1  # Skip }"
   416→            self.column += 1
   417→            value = ''.join(var_name)
   418→            self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STRING, value, self.line, start))
   419→        else:
   420→            # Single $ - treat as literal
   421→            self._add_token(TokenType.STRING, '$')
   422→
   423→    def _handle_number(self):
   424→        start = self.pos
   425→        has_dot = False
   426→
   427→        while self.pos < len(self.source) and (self.source[self.pos].isdigit() or self.source[self.pos] == '.'):
   428→            if self.source[self.pos] == '.':
   429→                if has_dot:
   430→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Multiple decimal points at line {self.line}")
   431→                has_dot = True
   432→            self.pos += 1
   433→            self.column += 1
   434→
   435→        value = self.source[start:self.pos]
   436→        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.NUMBER, value, self.line, start))
   437→
   438→    def _add_token(self, token_type: TokenType, value: str):
   439→        self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, self.line, self.column))
   440→        self.pos += 1
   441→        self.column += 1
   442→
   443→
   444→# =============================================================================
   445→# Parser
   446→# =============================================================================
   447→
   448→class Parser:
   449→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token]):
   450→        self.tokens = tokens
   451→        self.pos = 0
   452→
   453→    def parse(self) -> Dict[str, Task]:
   454→        tasks = {}
   455→
   456→        while self.pos < len(self.tokens) and self.tokens[self.pos].type != TokenType.EOF:
   457→            if self.check(TokenType.TASK):
   458→                task = self.parse_task()
   459→                tasks[task.name] = task
   460→            else:
   461→                self.raise_error(f"Expected 'task', got {self.current().value}")
   462→
   463→        return tasks
   464→
   465→    def parse_task(self) -> Task:
   466→        self.expect(TokenType.TASK)
   467→        name = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   468→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   469→
   470→        params = []
   471→        run = []
   472→        success: Dict[str, Expression] = {}
   473→        requires: Optional[RequiresBlock] = None
   474→        output = None
   475→        timeout = None
   476→
   477→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   478→            if self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   479→                field_name = self.current().value
   480→
   481→                # Check if there's a colon after (optional)
   482→                has_colon = self.match(TokenType.COLON)
   483→
   484→                if field_name == 'params':
   485→                    params = self.parse_params()
   486→                elif field_name == 'run':
   487→                    run = self.parse_run_block()
   488→                elif field_name == 'success':
   489→                    success = self.parse_success()
   490→                elif field_name == 'requires':
   491→                    requires = self.parse_requires()
   492→                elif field_name == 'output':
   493→                    output = self.parse_string_or_expr_value()
   494→                elif field_name == 'timeout':
   495→                    timeout = self.parse_number_value()
   496→                else:
   497→                    self.raise_error(f"Unknown field '{field_name}'")
   498→            else:
   499→                self.raise_error(f"Expected identifier, got {self.current().value}")
   500→
   501→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   502→        return Task(name=name, params=params, run=run, success=success,
   503→                   requires=requires, output=output, timeout=timeout)
   504→
   505→    def parse_params(self) -> List[Param]:
   506→        params = []
   507→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   508→
   509→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   510→            if self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   511→                name = self.current().value
   512→                if self.match(TokenType.COLON):
   513→                    # x: int syntax
   514→                    type_token = self.expect(TokenType.PARAM_TYPE)
   515→                    type_name = type_token.value
   516→                    default = None
   517→                    has_default = False
   518→
   519→                    # Check for optional = default
   520→                    if self.match(TokenType.ASSIGN):
   521→                        has_default = True
   522→                        default = self.parse_default_value(type_name)
   523→
   524→                    params.append(Param(name=name, type=type_name, default=default,
   525→                                      has_default=has_default))
   526→                else:
   527→                    self.raise_error("Expected ':' after parameter name")
   528→            elif self.match(TokenType.COMMA):
   529→                continue
   530→            else:
   531→                break
   532→
   533→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   534→        return params
   535→
   536→    def parse_default_value(self, expected_type: str) -> Any:
   537→        if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   538→            val = self.current().value
   539→            if expected_type not in ['string', 'list']:
   540→                self.raise_error(f"String value not expected for type {expected_type}")
   541→            return val
   542→        elif self.match(TokenType.NUMBER):
   543→            val = self.current().value
   544→            if expected_type == 'int':
   545→                return int(float(val))
   546→            elif expected_type == 'float':
   547→                return float(val)
   548→            else:
   549→                self.raise_error(f"Number value not expected for type {expected_type}")
   550→        elif self.match(TokenType.BOOL):
   551→            val = self.current().value == 'TRUE'
   552→            if expected_type != 'bool':
   553→                self.raise_error(f"Bool value not expected for type {expected_type}")
   554→            return val
   555→        elif self.match(TokenType.LBRACKET):
   556→            # Parse list
   557→            items = []
   558→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACKET):
   559→                if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   560→                    items.append(self.current().value)
   561→                elif self.match(TokenType.NUMBER):
   562→                    items.append(self.current().value)
   563→                elif self.match(TokenType.BOOL):
   564→                    items.append(self.current().value == 'TRUE')
   565→                else:
   566→                    self.raise_error("Invalid list element")
   567→                if not self.match(TokenType.COMMA):
   568→                    break
   569→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACKET)
   570→            return items
   571→        else:
   572→            self.raise_error(f"Unexpected default value: {self.current().value}")
   573→
   574→    def parse_run_block(self) -> List[str]:
   575→        """Parse run block content - just collect strings"""
   576→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   577→        lines = []
   578→
   579→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   580→            if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   581→                lines.append(self.current().value)
   582→            elif self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   583→                # Handle identifiers that might be part of command
   584→                lines.append(self.current().value)
   585→            else:
   586→                self.advance()
   587→
   588→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   589→        return lines
   590→
   591→    def parse_string_or_expr_value(self) -> str:
   592→        """Parse a string or expression value"""
   593→        if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   594→            return self.current().value
   595→        self.raise_error("Expected string value")
   596→
   597→    def parse_success(self) -> Dict[str, Expression]:
   598→        success = {}
   599→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   600→
   601→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   602→            name = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   603→            self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   604→
   605→            # Parse block of expressions
   606→            expressions = []
   607→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   608→                expr = self.parse_expression()
   609→                expressions.append(expr)
   610→                if self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON):
   611→                    pass
   612→
   613→            success[name] = self._make_block_expression(expressions)
   614→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   615→
   616→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   617→        return success
   618→
   619→    def _make_block_expression(self, expressions: List[Expression]) -> Expression:
   620→        """Combine multiple expressions in a block"""
   621→        if len(expressions) == 0:
   622→            return Literal(True)
   623→        # Last expression is the return value
   624→        return expressions[-1]
   625→
   626→    def parse_requires(self) -> RequiresBlock:
   627→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   628→        expressions = []
   629→
   630→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.EOF):
   631→            expr = self.parse_expression()
   632→            expressions.append(expr)
   633→            if self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON):
   634→                pass
   635→            elif not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   636→                pass
   637→
   638→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   639→        return RequiresBlock(expressions=expressions)
   640→
   641→    def parse_expression(self) -> Expression:
   642→        if self.match(TokenType.FOR):
   643→            return self.parse_for_loop()
   644→        elif self.match(TokenType.WHILE):
   645→            return self.parse_while_loop()
   646→        elif self.match(TokenType.IF):
   647→            return self.parse_if_block()
   648→        elif self.match(TokenType.RETURN):
   649→            return self.parse_return()
   650→        elif self.match(TokenType.BREAK):
   651→            return BreakStmt()
   652→        elif self.match(TokenType.CONTINUE):
   653→            return ContinueStmt()
   654→        elif self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   655→            name = self.current().value
   656→            if self.match(TokenType.LPAREN):
   657→                return self.parse_function_call(name)
   658→            elif self.match(TokenType.ASSIGN):
   659→                value = self.parse_expression()
   660→                return Assignment(var_name=name, value=value)
   661→            else:
   662→                # Could be a function call without parens or a variable reference
   663→                if self.match(TokenType.DOT):
   664→                    return self.parse_function_call(name)
   665→                return VariableRef(name=name)
   666→        elif self.match(TokenType.LBRACE):
   667→            # Block of expressions
   668→            expressions = []
   669→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   670→                expr = self.parse_expression()
   671→                expressions.append(expr)
   672→                self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   673→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   674→            return self._make_block_expression(expressions)
   675→        elif self.match(TokenType.NOT):
   676→            operand = self.parse_expression()
   677→            return UnaryOp(op='!', operand=operand)
   678→        elif self.match(TokenType.LPAREN):
   679→            expr = self.parse_expression()
   680→            self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   681→            return expr
   682→        else:
   683→            # Could be binary operation
   684→            return self.parse_binary_op()
   685→
   686→    def parse_for_loop(self) -> ForLoop:
   687→        self.expect(TokenType.LPAREN)
   688→        var_name = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   689→        self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)  # '='
   690→        self.expect(TokenType.NUMBER)  # start value
   691→        start_val = self.current().value
   692→        self.expect(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   693→        cond = self.parse_expression()
   694→        self.expect(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   695→        self.expect(TokenType.NUMBER)  # increment value
   696→        increment_val = self.current().value
   697→        self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   698→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   699→
   700→        body = []
   701→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   702→            body.append(self.parse_expression())
   703→            self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   704→
   705→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   706→
   707→        # Simplified: just track the pattern
   708→        start_expr = Literal(int(start_val))
   709→        end_expr = Literal(int(increment_val))  # This should be the end condition
   710→        return ForLoop(var_name=var_name, start=start_expr, end=end_expr, body=body)
   711→
   712→    def parse_while_loop(self) -> Expression:
   713→        self.expect(TokenType.LPAREN)
   714→        cond = self.parse_expression()
   715→        self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   716→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   717→
   718→        body = []
   719→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   720→            body.append(self.parse_expression())
   721→            self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   722→
   723→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   724→        return ForLoop(var_name="", start=cond, end=None, body=body)  # Simplified
   725→
   726→    def parse_if_block(self) -> IfBlock:
   727→        self.expect(TokenType.LPAREN)
   728→        cond = self.parse_expression()
   729→        self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   730→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   731→
   732→        then_body = []
   733→        while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self.check(TokenType.ELIF):
   734→            then_body.append(self.parse_expression())
   735→            self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   736→
   737→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   738→
   739→        elif_blocks = []
   740→        else_body = None
   741→
   742→        while self.match(TokenType.ELIF):
   743→            self.expect(TokenType.LPAREN)
   744→            elif_cond = self.parse_expression()
   745→            self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   746→            self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   747→
   748→            elif_body = []
   749→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   750→                elif_body.append(self.parse_expression())
   751→                self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   752→
   753→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   754→            elif_blocks.append((elif_cond, elif_body))
   755→
   756→        if self.match(TokenType.ELSE):
   757→            self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   758→            else_body = []
   759→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACE):
   760→                else_body.append(self.parse_expression())
   761→                self.match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
   762→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   763→
   764→        return IfBlock(condition=cond, then_body=then_body, elif_blocks=elif_blocks,
   765→                      else_body=else_body)
   766→
   767→    def parse_return(self) -> ReturnStmt:
   768→        value = self.parse_expression()
   769→        return ReturnStmt(value=value)
   770→
   771→    def parse_function_call(self, name: str) -> Expression:
   772→        args = []
   773→        if not self.check(TokenType.RPAREN):
   774→            while True:
   775→                # Handle named arguments: x=10
   776→                if self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   777→                    arg_name = self.current().value
   778→                    if self.match(TokenType.EQUALS):
   779→                        arg_value = self.parse_expression()
   780→                        args.append((arg_name, arg_value))
   781→                    else:
   782→                        args.append(VariableRef(name=arg_name))
   783→                else:
   784→                    arg_value = self.parse_expression()
   785→                    args.append(arg_value)
   786→
   787→                if not self.match(TokenType.COMMA):
   788→                    break
   789→
   790→        self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   791→
   792→        if name in ['exists', 'contains', 'equals']:
   793→            if len(args) < 2:
   794→                self.raise_error(f"Function {name} requires at least 2 arguments")
   795→            if name == 'exists':
   796→                return ExistsCall(path=args[0])
   797→            elif name == 'contains':
   798→                return ContainsCall(file_or_stream=args[0], pattern=args[1])
   799→            elif name == 'equals':
   800→                return EqualsCall(left=args[0], right=args[1])
   801→        elif name == 'fail':
   802→            if len(args) != 1:
   803→                self.raise_error("fail() requires exactly 1 argument")
   804→            return FailCall(task_name=args[0])
   805→
   806→        # Generic function call
   807→        return FunctionCall(name=name, args=[a if not isinstance(a, tuple) else a[1] for a in args])
   808→
   809→    def parse_binary_op(self) -> Expression:
   810→        left = self.parse_term()
   811→
   812→        while True:
   813→            op_type = self.current().type
   814→            if op_type in [TokenType.PLUS, TokenType.MINUS, TokenType.MULT, TokenType.DIV,
   815→                          TokenType.EQ, TokenType.NEQ, TokenType.LT, TokenType.GT,
   816→                          TokenType.LTE, TokenType.GTE, TokenType.AND, TokenType.OR,
   817→                          TokenType.PERCENT]:
   818→                op = self.current().value
   819→                self.advance()
   820→                right = self.parse_term()
   821→                left = BinaryOp(op=op, left=left, right=right)
   822→            else:
   823→                break
   824→
   825→        return left
   826→
   827→    def parse_term(self) -> Expression:
   828→        if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   829→            return Literal(value=self.current().value)
   830→        elif self.match(TokenType.NUMBER):
   831→            val = self.current().value
   832→            if '.' in val:
   833→                return Literal(value=float(val))
   834→            return Literal(value=int(val))
   835→        elif self.match(TokenType.BOOL):
   836→            return Literal(value=self.current().value == 'TRUE')
   837→        elif self.match(TokenType.LBRACKET):
   838→            items = []
   839→            while not self.check(TokenType.RBRACKET):
   840→                if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   841→                    items.append(self.current().value)
   842→                elif self.match(TokenType.NUMBER):
   843→                    items.append(float(self.current().value) if '.' in self.current().value else int(self.current().value))
   844→                elif self.match(TokenType.BOOL):
   845→                    items.append(self.current().value == 'TRUE')
   846→                if self.match(TokenType.COMMA):
   847→                    pass
   848→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACKET)
   849→            return Literal(value=items)
   850→        elif self.match(TokenType.LPAREN):
   851→            expr = self.parse_expression()
   852→            self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   853→            return expr
   854→        elif self.match(TokenType.IDENTIFIER):
   855→            name = self.current().value
   856→            if self.match(TokenType.DOT):
   857→                # Handle field access like stdout, stderr
   858→                field = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   859→                return BinaryOp(op='.', left=VariableRef(name=name), right=Literal(value=field))
   860→            return VariableRef(name=name)
   861→        elif self.match(TokenType.MINUS):
   862→            operand = self.parse_term()
   863→            return UnaryOp(op='-', operand=operand)
   864→        else:
   865→            self.raise_error(f"Unexpected token: {self.current().value}")
   866→
   867→    def parse_string_value(self) -> str:
   868→        if self.match(TokenType.STRING):
   869→            return self.current().value
   870→        self.raise_error("Expected string value")
   871→
   872→    def parse_number_value(self) -> float:
   873→        if self.match(TokenType.NUMBER):
   874→            return float(self.current().value)
   875→        self.raise_error("Expected number value")
   876→
   877→    # Helper methods
   878→    def current(self) -> Token:
   879→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   880→
   881→    def check(self, token_type: TokenType) -> bool:
   882→        return self.tokens[self.pos].type == token_type
   883→
   884→    def match(self, token_type: TokenType) -> bool:
   885→        if self.check(token_type):
   886→            self.advance()
   887→            return True
   888→        return False
   889→
   890→    def expect(self, token_type: TokenType) -> Token:
   891→        token = self.current()
   892→        if token.type != token_type:
   893→            self.raise_error(f"Expected {token_type}, got {token.type}: {token.value}")
   894→        self.advance()
   895→        return token
   896→
   897→    def advance(self):
   898→        if self.pos < len(self.tokens) - 1:
   899→            self.pos += 1
   900→
   901→    def raise_error(self, msg: str):
   902→        token = self.current()
   903→        raise SyntaxError(f"Line {token.line}: {msg}")

   909→
   910→@dataclass
   911→class Config:
   912→    entry: Optional[str] = None
   913→    clean_cwd: bool = False
   914→    env: Dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
   915→
   916→
   917→def parse_config(path: Optional[str]) -> Config:
   918→    if path is None or not os.path.exists(path):
   919→        return Config()
   920→
   921→    with open(path, 'r') as f:
   922→        data = toml.load(f)
   923→
   924→    config = Config()
   925→
   926→    if 'entry' in data:
   927→        config.entry = str(data['entry'])
   928→
   929→    if 'clean_cwd' in data:
   930→        config.clean_cwd = bool(data['clean_cwd'])
   931→
   932→    if 'env' in data:
   933→        config.env = {k: str(v) for k, v in data['env'].items()}
   934→
   935→    return config

   938→# =============================================================================
   939→# Expression Evaluator
   940→# =============================================================================
   941→
   942→@dataclass
   943→class EvaluationContext:
   944→    params: Dict[str, Any]
   945→    variables: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
   946→    workspace: str = ""
   947→    stdout: str = ""
   948→    stderr: str = ""
   949→    exit_code: int = 0
   950→    file_exists_func = None
   951→
   952→
   953→class ExpressionEvaluator:
   954→    def __init__(self, context: EvaluationContext):
   955→        self.context = context
   956→
   957→    def evaluate(self, expr: Expression) -> Any:
   958→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   959→            return expr.value
   960→        elif isinstance(expr, VariableRef):
   961→            if expr.name in self.context.variables:
   962→                return self.context.variables[expr.name]
   963→            elif expr.name == 'stdout':
   964→                return self.context.stdout
   965→            elif expr.name == 'stderr':
   966→                return self.context.stderr
   967→            elif expr.name == 'exit_code':
   968→                return self.context.exit_code
   969→            raise ValueError(f"Undefined variable: {expr.name}")
   970→        elif isinstance(expr, BinaryOp):
   971→            return self._eval_binary_op(expr)
   972→        elif isinstance(expr, UnaryOp):
   973→            left = self.evaluate(expr.operand)
   974→            if expr.op == '!':
   975→                return not left
   976→            elif expr.op == '-':
   977→                return -left
   978→        elif isinstance(expr, ForLoop):
   979→            return self._eval_for_loop(expr)
   980→        elif isinstance(expr, IfBlock):
   981→            return self._eval_if_block(expr)
   982→        elif isinstance(expr, ReturnStmt):
   983→            return self.evaluate(expr.value)
   984→        elif isinstance(expr, Assignment):
   985→            value = self.evaluate(expr.value)
   986→            self.context.variables[expr.var_name] = value
   987→            return value
   988→        elif isinstance(expr, FunctionCall):
   989→            return self._eval_function_call(expr)
   990→        elif isinstance(expr, ExistsCall):
   991→            path = self.evaluate(expr.path)
   992→            return os.path.exists(path)
   993→        elif isinstance(expr, ContainsCall):
   994→            content = self.evaluate(expr.file_or_stream)
   995→            pattern = self.evaluate(expr.pattern)
   996→            return pattern in content
   997→        elif isinstance(expr, EqualsCall):
   998→            left = self.evaluate(expr.left)
   999→            right = self.evaluate(expr.right)
  1000→            return left == right
  1001→        elif isinstance(expr, FailCall):
  1002→            # Special handling for fail() - just return True (placeholder)
  1003→            return True
  1004→
  1005→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
  1006→
  1007→    def _eval_binary_op(self, expr: BinaryOp) -> Any:
  1008→        left = self.evaluate(expr.left)
  1009→        right = self.evaluate(expr.right)
  1010→
  1011→        if expr.op == '+':
  1012→            return left + right
  1013→        elif expr.op == '-':
  1014→            return left - right
  1015→        elif expr.op == '*':
  1016→            return left * right
  1017→        elif expr.op == '/':
  1018→            return left / right
  1019→        elif expr.op == '%':
  1020→            return str(left) + str(right)  # String concatenation
  1021→        elif expr.op == '==':
  1022→            return left == right
  1023→        elif expr.op == '!=':
  1024→            return left != right
  1025→        elif expr.op == '<':
  1026→            return left < right
  1027→        elif expr.op == '>':
  1028→            return left > right
  1029→        elif expr.op == '<=':
  1030→            return left <= right
  1031→        elif expr.op == '>=':
  1032→            return left >= right
  1033→        elif expr.op == 'and':
  1034→            return left and right
  1035→        elif expr.op == 'or':
  1036→            return left or right
  1037→
  1038→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown operator: {expr.op}")
  1039→
  1040→    def _eval_for_loop(self, expr: ForLoop) -> Any:
  1041→        result = None
  1042→        if expr.start is not None and expr.end is not None:
  1043→            start = self.evaluate(expr.start) if isinstance(expr.start, Literal) else 0
  1044→            end = self.evaluate(expr.end) if isinstance(expr.end, Literal) else 10
  1045→            for i in range(start, end):
  1046→                self.context.variables[expr.var_name] = i
  1047→                for stmt in expr.body:
  1048→                    if isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
  1049→                        result = self.evaluate(stmt)
  1050→                    elif isinstance(stmt, BreakStmt):
  1051→                        return result
  1052→                    elif isinstance(stmt, ContinueStmt):
  1053→                        continue
  1054→                    else:
  1055→                        self.evaluate(stmt)
  1056→        return result or True
  1057→
  1058→    def _eval_if_block(self, expr: IfBlock) -> Any:
  1059→        cond = self.evaluate(expr.condition)
  1060→        if cond:
  1061→            result = None
  1062→            for stmt in expr.then_body:
  1063→                if isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
  1064→                    result = self.evaluate(stmt)
  1065→                else:
  1066→                    self.evaluate(stmt)
  1067→            return result
  1068→
  1069→        for elif_cond, elif_body in expr.elif_blocks:
  1070→            if self.evaluate(elif_cond):
  1071→                result = None
  1072→                for stmt in elif_body:
  1073→                    if isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
  1074→                        result = self.evaluate(stmt)
  1075→                    else:
  1076→                        self.evaluate(stmt)
  1077→                return result
  1078→
  1079→        if expr.else_body:
  1080→            result = None
  1081→            for stmt in expr.else_body:
  1082→                if isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
  1083→                    result = self.evaluate(stmt)
  1084→                else:
  1085→                    self.evaluate(stmt)
  1086→            return result
  1087→
  1088→        return False
  1089→
  1090→    def _eval_function_call(self, expr: FunctionCall) -> Any:
  1091→        evalled_args = [self.evaluate(arg) if not isinstance(arg, tuple) else
  1092→                       self.evaluate(arg[1]) for arg in expr.args]
  1093→        return evalled_args[0] if evalled_args else None
  1094→

        if self.minutes is not None:

        if self.hours is not None:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Pipeline execution CLI tool.
Executes pipelines from a pipeline file and an optional TOML config file.
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys
import time
import toml
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
from pathlib import Path
import shlex


# =============================================================================
# Data Structures
# =============================================================================

class TaskStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    RUNNING = "RUNNING"
    COMPLETED = "COMPLETED"
    FAILED = "FAILED"
